attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00236 — SEQ is derived from source CIA-036 and is provisional; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/assign the real sequence within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket.
Patient sex_at_birth inferred as female (source note does not state sex) — flag for clinician confirmation; diagnosis and management are sex-independent.
Patient age inferred as 24 ('young adult' in source, no exact age given) — flag for clinician confirmation; adult age band, no paediatric dosing concern.
Geographic remoteness not stated in source (private residence, urban after-hours home-visit context) — geographic_context omitted rather than guessed.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person urgent after-hours HOME visit with a full dermatological examination. Exam-only findings (collarette scaling, Christmas-tree cleavage-line distribution, cervical lymphadenopathy) and clinician-measured vitals were sealed into nodes 10/11 with telehealth_limits; only patient-reportable/video-observable data placed in 01.objective_data_offered.

Tier calibration: source clinician framed an urgent after-hours MBS 585-600 home visit, but the note's own Risk Level is 'Low' and the stated goal was to AVERT unnecessary ED presentation. Baseline tier set to T1 (routine GP confirmation + home self-care) to reflect true low acuity; over-escalation (T3+) is encoded as the moderate over-triage failure mode.

AMT product codes for cetirizine, emollient and hydrocortisone were NOT assigned (no confident AMT code available without a terminology receipt) — omitted rather than fabricated; capture at ingest terminology-verification.
Differential SNOMED codes (secondary syphilis, guttate psoriasis, tinea corporis, drug eruption) are candidate/unverified and registered in codes_manifest for terminology verification.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
